records.ety: [OF. doctur, L. doctor, teacher, fr. docere to teach. See Docile.]

defs: A teacher; one skilled in a profession, or branch of knowledge; a learned man.
quotes: One of the doctors of Italy, Nicholas Macciavel.

defs: An academical title, originally meaning a man so well versed in his department as to be qualified to teach it. Hence: One who has taken the highest degree conferred by a university or college, or has received a diploma of the highest degree; as, a doctor of divinity, of law, of medicine, of music, or of philosophy. Such diplomas may confer an honorary title only.
One duly licensed to practice medicine; a member of the medical profession; a physician.
quotes: By medicine life may be prolonged, yet death Will seize the doctor too.

defs: Any mechanical contrivance intended to remedy a difficulty or serve some purpose in an exigency; as, the doctor of a calico-printing machine, which is a knife to remove superfluous coloring matter; the doctor, or auxiliary engine, called also donkey engine.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Doctored (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Doctoring.]

defs: To treat as a physician does; to apply remedies to; to repair; as, to doctor a sick man or a broken cart.
To confer a doctorate upon; to make a doctor.
To tamper with and arrange for one's own purposes; to falsify; to adulterate; as, to doctor election returns; to doctor whisky.
